organization: Ohio University - Dr. Shawn Ostermann & Ilya Kogan
description:
Leading the integration of **Backstage** (an internal developer portal) into the university's cloud-native **Kubernetes** platform, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and developer workflow improvements.

- Implemented **Azure AD SSO**, Microsoft Graph API user provisioning, and secure authentication flows
- Integrated platform tooling including **Jira**, Kubernetes metrics & monitoring, TechDocs, GitHub org imports, and catalog templates
- Working in a Linux-based cloud environment using containers, cloud APIs, and infrastructure automation
- Upcoming work includes enabling GitHub-based student login and expanding platform access

role: Instructor of Record — CS1400: Fundamentals of Computing
description:
Sole instructor for an introductory computing course of ~25 students, responsible for the full instructional lifecycle from curriculum design to final grading.

- Designed and delivered lectures, quizzes, programming assignments, exams, and supplemental materials
- Taught foundational concepts including computational thinking, problem solving, algorithms, **Python** programming, and contemporary issues in computer science
- Provided timely, constructive feedback to support student growth and learning outcomes

Conducted systems and networking research focused on evaluating and optimizing the performance of **ION** (a delay-tolerant networking implementation) across diverse system conditions.

- Built and managed **QEMU** virtual machine environments to conduct controlled, reproducible systems tests
- Began developing a cloud-based automated testing framework using cloud platforms and configuration management tooling (**Ansible**, **Proxmox**)
- Deepened expertise in Linux internals, networking, virtualization, and high-availability system behavior

Worked within cross-functional teams to design, build, and maintain full-stack applications supporting university operations.

- Modernized and refactored legacy applications into current languages and platforms, improving maintainability and longevity
- Collaborated through **GitHub**, code reviews, and iterative development workflows using **React**, **Angular**, **Node.js**, and **TypeScript**

Supported districtwide technology infrastructure across multiple school buildings, contributing to day-to-day operational uptime and long-term hardware reliability.

- Handled device deployment, diagnostics, repairs, and classroom hardware installations
- Maintained networking equipment including switches and UPS systems
- Built hands-on experience in hardware troubleshooting, IT support workflows, and cross-team communication
